data.title: Dow, S&P, Nasdaq grasp for firm direction after volatile week
data.description:
Major market averages were mixed Friday, moving between small gains and losses after a volatile week that may leave the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) extending a run of weekly declines.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.1%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.1%. Six of the 11 S&P 500 sectors were higher, with the Consumer Discretionary group up 0.5% while the Consumer Staples group slipped about 0.1%.

The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq may notch a fourth week of losses in what was a wild week for equity and bond investors navigating Bank of Japan rate policy, disappointing Treasury auctions and a bigger-than-expected decline in weekly initial jobless claims.

"Financial markets have momentarily stabilized after concerns about hard-landing risks caused a brief Wile E. Coyote moment," economists at Bank of America said Friday. "Incoming data on initial claims and ISM Services reinforce our confidence that the US labor market is not rolling over. Yet markets continue to price in about twice as many cuts this year as we expect (~100bp versus 50bp, respectively)," they said.

"From here, the data will have to tell us what kind of economy we have: one that is slowing gradually or one that is slowing sharply," BofA said.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 6 basis points to 3.92%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 2 basis points to 4.03%.

Major U.S. stock indexes also ended higher on Thursday, as worries about the economy sliding into a recession were eased after the initial jobless claims came in less than expected.

Among individual movers, Akamai (AKAM) +10% after strong Q2 financial results from the cloud computing and security services company.

Expedia (EXPE) +8% after the online travel services company topped consensus estimates for Q2, with adjusted EBITDA up 5% at $786 million.

Insulet (PODD) -7% following mixed Q2 results.

data.title: S&P 500 stocks that return more capital than their cost of capital - BofA
data.description:
BofA Securities analysts listed S&P 500 (SP500) stocks that have the highest spread between return on capital and the cost of it.

Strategist Savita Subramanian wrote that the assumptions on the cost of capital have shifted from a negative real risk-free rate around the Covid pandemic to a positive one. Stock betas have also changed.

Analysts calculated the weighted average cost of capital (or WACC) based on the current equity risk premium of the S&P 500 (SP500), which is the forward earnings yield minus the 10-year real rates; the stock’s five-year beta, a 21% statutory tax rate, and the yield to maturity, based on the stock’s credit rating.

These are the stocks that can out-earn their cost of capital:

  • VeriSign Inc. (VRSN) - ROC minus WACC: 1103%
  • Fortinet Inc. (FTNT) - ROC minus WACC: 125%
  • Otis Worldwide Corp. (OTIS) - ROC minus WACC: 103%

data.title: Archer Aviation GAAP EPS of -$0.32 beats by $0.05
data.description:
• Archer Aviation press release (NYSE:ACHR): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.32 beats by $0.05.
  • Adjusted EBITDA loss increased by $7.0 million from an adjusted EBITDA loss of $86.8 million in the first quarter of 2024, primarily due to the increase in people related spending to support the Midnight program development and for our test and manufacturing capabilities.
  • We ended Q2’24 with $360.4 million of cash and cash equivalents on our balance sheet, with another $6.7 million of restricted cash. Since the end of Q2’24, we raised an additional $230 million in equity financing.

data.title: Kurita Water Industries reports Q1 results

  • Net sales of 94 billion yen (+3.8% Y/Y).

data.title: Archer Aviation drops after earnings release, Stellantis update
data.description:
Archer Aviation (NYSE:ACHR) fell 7.77% in late Friday morning trading after the company reported Q2 earnings results and updated on financial incentives with Stellantis (STLA).

The company reported a Q2 adjusted EBITDA loss of $93.8 million and an overall net loss of $106.9 million. Along with the earnings update, Archer Aviation (ACHR) announced that it has reached an agreement in principle on key terms of its planned contract manufacturing relationship with Stellantis (STLA). In addition to the nearly $300 million Stellantis (STLA) has previously invested to date, including $55 million in July, the automaker is planning to fund up to $390 million in manufacturing labor and capex to help ensure Archer (ACHR) achieves its manufacturing goals. Looking ahead, Archer (ACHR) intends to issue Stellantis $30 million of performance warrants that will vest based on Stellantis' (STLA) achievement of certain performance milestones under the contract manufacturing relationship. Archer's (ACHR) issuance of any equity to Stellantis (STLA) pursuant to the contract manufacturing relationship is expected to be subject to approval by shareholders.

Archer (ACHR) also unveiled plans for a Los Angeles air taxi network. The planned network includes vertiports at key locations such as Los Angeles International Airport, Orange County, Santa Monica, Hollywood Burbank, Long Beach, and Van Nuys.

ARK Invest has been an active buyer of Archer Aviation (ACHR) this year as the company has continued to strike eVTOL deals. On Seeking Alpha, Stone Fox Capital is a strong ACHR bull. "The key investor takeaway is that Archer Aviation is quickly approaching certification of their Midnight eVTOL and the launch of an air taxi service in multiple locations around the globe," wrote Stone Fox last month.

data.title: Genscript Biotech reports 1H results
data.description:
• Genscript Biotech press release (OTCPK:GNNSF): 1H revenue of approximately US$561.4 million, reflecting a robust 43.5% increase from US$391.3 million in the Prior Period.
  • Loss narrowed to approximately US$215.6 million, compared to US$245.8 million in the Prior Period. The adjusted net loss saw a significant improvement, decreasing to approximately US$69 million from US$162 million.

data.title: Bancolombia S.A. reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Bancolombia S.A. press release (NYSE:CIB): Q2 Net income attributable to shareholders of the parent company in the second quarter of 2024 was COP 1.4 trillion.
  • Gross loans amount to COP 268 trillion, a 3.0% growth compared to the previous quarter.
  • 30-day past-due loan ratio was 5.17% and the 90-day past-due loan ratio was 3.43%.

data.title: Perrigo recalls baby formula sold at CVS, H-E-B stores
data.description:
Perrigo (NYSE:PRGO) has voluntarily recalled 16,500 cans of its store brand Premium Infant Formula with Iron Milk-Based Powder that were shipped to H-E-B Grocery Company and CVS (CVS).

The healthcare products company said in a filing on Thursday that the recall was conducted on the retailer and warehouse level. The product was shipped to CVS between Feb. 6 and Feb. 11 and to H-E-B between Feb. 2 and May 13.

The recalled formula had been sent to H-E-B in Texas and to CVS in Texas, Florida, California, South Carolina, Virginia, Indiana, Tennessee, New Jersey, Michigan, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island and Missouri. Perrigo has asked H-E-B and CVS to examine their inventories and isolate the product.

According to a company statement posted on the FDA’s website, the product was voluntarily recalled after higher than permitted levels of Vitamin D were detected through routine testing. No reports of adverse events to date have been attributed to the elevated levels of Vitamin D.

Perrigo said in its filing that the recall is not expected to have a material impact on the company or the 2024 financial outlook it issued on Aug. 2.

data.title: Credicorp reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Credicorp press release (NYSE:BAP): Q2 NII increased 1.2% QoQ to stand at S/3,468 million.
  • Net Income attributable to Credicorp totaled S/1,339.1 million, which represented a drop of 11.4% QoQ and 4.4% YoY.
  • Core Income rose 3.9% QoQ and 10.5% YoY.
